steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Transitions question. The blank must name the logical relationship between the two sentences: sentence 1 says original ticker tape was retired long before 2019, and sentence 2 says something else was used at the 2019 parade instead. The relationship is simple sequence — what happened next.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — With this in mind,: signals that what follows is a decision or action taken because of the preceding information. The parade happening is not a reasoned response to ticker tape being retired. ✗
  • Choice B — In addition,: adds a parallel item to something already stated. The blank does not add another example of ticker tape use — it introduces a replacement. ✗
  • Choice C — Then,: signals the next event in a time sequence. After ticker tape was retired, the next thing that happened was shredded paper filled that role. Fits perfectly. ✓
  • Choice D — In other words,: introduces a restatement of what was just said. The second sentence is not a restatement — it introduces new information about what replaced ticker tape. ✗
That matches Choice C — Then,.
